title: World Environment Day: reconnecting with nature through culture
content:
A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment. 

The European Union supports these initiatives, helping transform ideas into projects with a lasting impact on people, places and nature. On World Environment Day, we put the spotlight on three Creative Europe-funded projects whose work is making a difference on the ground.

Working across coastal regions in Italy, Greece and Portugal, BLUETOUR brought together artists, cultural organisations and local communities to explore how tourism can help protect local heritage and ecosystems rather than put pressure on them.

The project resulted in a series of artworks rooted in local landscapes and identities. In Faro, the mural Cycles by Los Pepes Studio celebrates the rhythms of the Atlantic Ocean while, in Aegean Sea, artist Paolo Lolicata created Poetic Tour of Lesvos, an artistic map encouraging visitors to discover the island through its landscapes and literary heritage.

Together, these works invite residents and visitors alike to engage with places more thoughtfully and sustainably.
snippet: A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment.
